item.title: ChristianMahnke.de: The forgotten whalebones of Göttingen
item.description:
In 2017, I worked a lot of overtime and so spent half the spring in Levinschen Park (German)…

Ever since then, I’ve been wondering whether it might just be a whale’s lower jaw lying there, without anyone having noticed.

I was reminded of this again during the week and took a few photos. The exact location on various map providers.

Current status
Reconstruction
Detail 1: Porous structure (spongiosa)
Detail 2: Fibres

Now only half of the object remains. However, on Google Maps I found it looking just as I remember it:

  • Image from July 2021. On the right-hand edge of the picture.
  • Image from June 2022. Behind the red climbing frame on the right.

I have seen structures like this on several occasions, for example in North Frisia. Here you can see the much larger whale bone portal at the Dr Carl Häberlin Frisian Museum in Wyk on Föhr.

Wikimedia Commons contains many comparative images of arches made from baleen (more specifically: the lower jaws of baleen whales).

On closer inspection, even more details become apparent:

  • The material is fibrous in the upper layers and porous in the deeper layers.
  • Organic-looking, upward-facing openings are visible on the front, which are thought to be blood vessels.

There is no mention of it in the entry for Levinscher Park in the Lower Saxony Monument Atlas either.

The biologist and photographer Dirk Pfuhl regularly documents the wildlife of Levinschen Park and has also written about the history of the park, but there are no further clues to be found there either…

Perhaps it’s time to find out how the bones ended up there, and maybe put up a small information board as well.

item.title: ChristianMahnke.de: The art collection as a separate pages
item.description:
Until now, the art collection was only available as overviews under the relevant keywords…

The art collection is now available online, featuring all the works I still have. The gallery is presenting the so-called Petersburg hanging (German).

There are also galleries for different techniques:

  • Collages
  • Computer art
  • Paintings
  • Woodcuts
  • Linocuts
  • Lithographs
  • Photographs
  • Etchings
  • Screen prints
  • Drawings

In addition, the following regional groupings:

  • Göttingen
  • Lower Saxony

item.title: ChristianMahnke.de: Woodcut print of unknown origin
item.description:
I’ve found a colour woodcut by an unknown artist…

Print

The picture shows either the ruins of a castle or a castle perched on a hill overlooking a wide bend in the river. At the foot of the hill lies a small village by the river. In the foreground are a few conifers.

Although one might think that a relatively large castle overlooking a bend in the river would not be all that common, unfortunately no information has yet been found.

item.title: ChristianMahnke.de: Woodcut "Am Hang" ("On the Slope") by an unknown artist
item.description:
I’ve found a colour woodcut by an unknown artist…

Print
Signature

The picture shows a log cabin with small windows on a slope in a hilly landscape. A large deciduous tree towers above the house; it is this tree in particular that has slightly expressionist features.

The title of the print is “Am Hang”; it is designated as an O.-H. (original woodcut) and is signed “Jos. Winkelmann”.

Unfortunately, no further information about “Jos. Winkelmann” has yet been found.

item.title: Ric - An unknown artist: Hedwig Klinge Sonnenblume
item.description:
Kurze Beschreibung: Eine Radierung einer Sonnenblume von Hedwig Klinge, der Frau von Fritz Neumann.

Anmerkungen: Gefunden auf einem Flohmarkt in Hannover.

item.title: ChristianMahnke.de: Etching "Lillehaldjas" by Lüüdia Vallimäe-Mark (1925-2004)
item.description:
I’ve found a etching by Lüüdia Vallimäe-Mark (1925-2004) gefunden…

Print
Signature

Lüüdia Vallimäe-Mark (1925–2004) was an Estonian painter and graphic artist who trained at the historic Pallas Art School in Tartu. In addition to portraits and landscapes, she also created well-known illustrations for children’s books. However, little information is available about her graphic work.

The title of the print is “Lillehaldjas” (Flower Fairy in English); it dates from 1983 and is signed “L. Mark”.

item.title: Plastikwarenfabrik: LIDO XML zu HTML Konverter
item.description:
Auch wenn es 2026 gar kein Vibe Coding mehr gibt, hier ist der erste Eintrag in dieser neuen Kategorie.

Mit diesem kleinen Werkzeug können Sie LIDO-XML-Dateien, die Sie beispielsweise aus dem Sammlungsportal der Universität Göttingen exportiert haben, in HTML konvertieren. Im nächsten Schritt können die erzeugten Dateien z.B. im Arcana/RAG System der GWDG weiterverarbeitet zu werden.

Der Code ist bei GitHub unter cmahnke/lido2html verfügbar.

item.title: ChristianMahnke.de: Woodblock print "Dresden um 1900" by Erich Buchwald-Zinnwald (1884-1972)
item.description:
I bought a Woodblock print by Erich Buchwald-Zinnwald (in German) (1884-1972) (at artnet)…

Print
Signature

Erich Buchwald-Zinnwald (1884–1972) was a landscape painter and graphic artist, best known for his colour woodcuts of the Eastern Ore Mountains. Out of a sense of attachment to this region, he also adopted the surname “Zinnwald”. He was born in Dresden and moved to West Germany in 1968; from then on, he was regarded in the former GDR as a “Republikflüchtling” (refugee from the Republic).

The title of the print is “Dresden um 1900” (Dresden around 1900); it dates from 1952 and is inscribed with “Original woodcut” and the signature “E. Buchwald Zinnwald”. A similar woodcut by Buchwald-Zinnwald is listed at MutualArt.

item.title: Kleiderbügel: Sorge
item.description: Bekleidungshaus SORGE Düsseldorf Kauf ohne Sorge bei S O R G E

item.title: ChristianMahnke.de: NPM Organisation
item.description:
I’ve created an NPM “organisation”.

So far, I’ve only published two JavaScript artefacts:

  • The JavaScript library for HDR
  • The Rollup plugin for WASM inlining

However, in order to better manage more or less internal tools and modules, these are now also being published as NPM packages. As the GitHub internal package registry requires authentication even for public packages, it is too complex to integrate for my purposes.

Consequently, the ‘organisation’ @projektemacher has been created to bring everything together under one roof.

The hdr-canvas package will therefore be moved in the next version.

item.title: Plastikwarenfabrik: Abkürzungsgenerator
item.description:
Kein gutes Projekt kann ohne einen Kurz- bzw. Projektnamen auskommen. Er beschreibt das Ziel, ist leicht zu merken und wirkt somit identitätsstiftend für alle Beteiligten.

Mit diesem Prompt kann man z.B. Abkürzungen für ein Webseitenprojekt auf Basis von Kubernetes erstellen:

Erstelle mehrere Akronyme für ein Webprojekt, bei denen „K“ für Kubernetes steht. Die Akronyme sollen gleichzeitig echte Namen aus der griechischen Mythologie sein (z. B. Götter, Titanen, Helden).

Bedingungen:

  • Fokus auf Web/Website (Begriffe wie Web, Online, Sites, Internet müssen im Akronym vorkommen)
  • Kein Bezug zu AI
  • Für jeden Vorschlag: 0. Akronym + ausgeschriebene Bedeutung 0. Kurze Erklärung der mythologischen Figur dahinter

Halte die Vorschläge kurz, klar und möglichst natürlich klingend (keine zu erzwungenen Backronyme).

Antworten von QWEN3

1. KRONOS – Kubernetes Realms Online Network System
Mythologische Figur: Kronos – Titan der Zeit, Vater der Götter. Er symbolisiert die kontinuierliche, strukturierte Bewegung von Ereignissen.
Erklärung: Wie Kronos die Zeit regiert, so verwaltet das System die kontinuierliche Bereitstellung und Skalierung von Web-Infrastrukturen – stetig, zuverlässig, im Rhythmus der Digitalität.

2. KALLISTO – Kubernetes Advanced Live Linking System for Online Tech
Mythologische Figur: Kallisto – eine der Titaninnen, Tochter von Titan Iapetus. Sie wurde von Zeus geliebt und in die Sterne erhoben.
Erklärung: Kallisto steht für Schönheit und Stabilität im Himmel – ebenso wie das Projekt, das Webseiten elegant, sicher und stetig online hält.

3. KEPHALOS – Kubernetes Engine for Public High-Performance Online Sites
Mythologische Figur: Kephalos – ein berühmter Jäger und Held, der durch seine Präzision und Weisheit auffiel.
Erklärung: Wie Kephalos den richtigen Pfeil trifft, so sorgt das System für präzise, schnelle und stabile Web-Auslieferung – ohne Abweichung.

4. KLEOS – Kubernetes Layer for Efficient Online Systems
Mythologische Figur: Kleos – das griechische Wort für „Ruhm“ oder „ewige Anerkennung“, oft mit Helden verbunden.
Erklärung: Ein Webprojekt, das durch Kubernetes stabil und leistungsstark läuft, erlangt selbst „Kleos“ – Anerkennung in der digitalen Welt.

5. KORYBANTIS – Kubernetes Orchestrated Responsive Yield for Browsers & Sites
Mythologische Figur: Korybanten – die tanzenden, rituellen Krieger, die den jungen Zeus beschützten.
Erklärung: Wie die Korybanten den Himmel mit Lärm und Energie erfüllten, so sorgt das System für dynamische, reaktive Web-Performance – laut, aber geordnet.

item.title: ChristianMahnke.de: Fediverse integration for development platforms
item.description:
GitHub is currently facing a lot of issues – what does it take to switch?

A great deal has already been written about the technical issues (reliability, security problems, etc.), and numerous videos have been produced. There is also plenty of information available on the topics of digital sovereignty and independence from Microsoft.

To cut a long story short: there are good reasons to look for alternatives to GitHub. And at first glance, there is a fair selection available:

  • GitLab
  • Codeberg
  • Gitea
  • Forgejo
  • Bitbucket

Some of these offer only hosting services, whilst others also provide the software for local installation.

However, hosting services have a few drawbacks compared to GitHub (at least if you have an old account). GitHub offers a vast array of resources, orders of magnitude more than the competition:

  • Storage space: Particularly with my image-heavy blogs, a repository can sometimes run to several gigabytes in size. There are also the odd files that are around 50 MB in size.
  • Container images of a few gigabytes in size can also be stored.
  • The runners have a maximum runtime of six hours and provide a virtual machine which, with a bit of fiddling, offers a mid-double-digit gigabyte amount of free space, thereby also allowing for extensive data transformations.

In addition, there are CI infrastructures and other automation tools that can be set up on GitHub.

Another factor arising from the platform’s scale is the network effect, which receives little attention. In my view, this is a major oversight if one is seriously advocating for a replacement.

GitHub is a social network

Simply because so many projects, particularly from the JavaScript and TypeScript communities, can now be found on GitHub, they are interconnected not only in terms of software but also in terms of the people involved. Although these are primarily working relationships, one should not forget that a simple @ mention in an issue or comment can be used to draw other people’s attention to something. The same applies to linking information between projects. GitHub also recognises these links and integrates them into various history views (issues, comments, discussions, pull requests, etc.).

In other words: GitHub is a social network, and so for many people, concerns about sovereignty or dependence on the big tech giant Microsoft are simply not a sufficient reason to leave this convenient ecosystem.

The prospect of having to maintain their code on a small island, cut off from their community, is unlikely to inspire much enthusiasm for switching. For smaller projects in particular, the network effect also means potentially increased visibility – for example, when maintaining one’s own projects to improve one’s prospects on the job market.

Development platforms must join the Fediverse!

Therefore, the following applies: anyone who is serious about reducing the use of GitHub or replacing it with decentralised European solutions or providers must advocate for the interconnection of platforms. Digital sovereignty alone isn’t really that appealing.

ActivityPub (the technical foundation of the Fediverse) provides all the necessary mechanisms. Every platform must be able to address at least the following entities:

  • Users
  • Issues / Discussions – and comments where applicable
  • Pull / Merge Requests
  • Tags / Releases

At the presentation level, the platforms must then provide various ‘inboxes’ and display them in the appropriate contexts, e.g. notifications or issue discussions for users.

Meanwhile, the platforms also offer additional features such as simple project management or boards, and documentation or wikis. But let’s start with the basics first.

The idea isn’t new; there’s ForgeFed, for example, but the integration has been fairly limited so far. Instead, the focus tends to be on hype topics such as “Agentic AI”. These certainly have their place, particularly when you need to attract customers using buzzwords (e.g. organisations as). But you won’t reach (informal open-source) communities that way.

Anyone who wants a future of open-source collaboration free from dependence on Big Tech must not treat interconnectivity as a mere additional feature, but must recognise it as an infrastructural necessity.
